title: Maintenance Fabricator
description: **Maintenance Fabricator (Welding Essential)** Nature’s Best Ltd is Ireland’s leading producer of fresh salads and chilled convenience foods. Based in Drogheda. We are now looking for a **skilled and experienced Maintenance Fabricator** to join our dynamic engineering team. If you’re passionate about welding, stainless steel fabrication, and delivering high\-quality workmanship in a fast\-paced manufacturing environment, this is an exciting opportunity to build a long\-term career with a market leader. **Your Role** As a **Maintenance Fabricator**, you’ll play a vital role in maintaining and improving our production equipment, ensuring reliability, efficiency, and high engineering standards throughout the facility. **Key Responsibilities** * **Fabrication \& Repair** – Carry out high\-quality fabrication and welding repairs on machinery, pipework, and structural components. * **Stainless Steel Welding** – Perform precision TIG/MIG/stick welding on stainless steel equipment and pipe systems. * **Pipe Purging \& Finishing** – Must be able to purge weld stainless steel pipes to food\-grade standards. * **High\-Quality Polishing** – Polish stainless steel surfaces to a high\-quality hygienic finish suitable for food production environments. * **Sheet Metal Fabrication** – Operate manual folders and carry out folding of sheeting accurately and efficiently. * **Preventative Maintenance** – Conduct scheduled mechanical maintenance to minimise downtime and improve equipment performance. * **Problem Solving** – Diagnose and resolve mechanical and fabrication issues quickly and effectively. * **Engineering Collaboration** – Work closely with engineers, maintenance fitters, and production teams. * **Health \& Safety** – Maintain high safety standards and ensure all work complies with company and industry regulations. **What We’re Looking For** * **Proven Welding Experience** – Strong MIG, TIG, and Arc welding skills are essential. * **Welding Certifications Required** – Relevant welding courses and certifications must be held. * **Stainless Steel Experience** – Experience working with stainless steel fabrication, polishing, and hygienic welding practices. * **Pipework Knowledge** – Ability to purge weld stainless steel pipe systems to a high standard. * **Fabrication Skills** – Experience using manual folders and sheet metal fabrication equipment. * **Technical Understanding** – Ability to read and interpret engineering drawings and technical specifications. * **CAD Knowledge Preferred** – Familiarity with CAD systems and technical drawings would be an advantage. * **Mechanical Aptitude** – Strong background in mechanical fitting, fabrication, and machine repair. * **Team Player** – Good communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively. * **Flexible \& Reliable** – Willingness to work shifts, weekends, and respond to breakdowns when required. * **Food Industry Experience** – Previous experience in food manufacturing or a similar production environment is desirable. **What We Offer** * **Competitive Salary** based on experience and skill level. * **Training \& Upskilling Opportunities** to support your long\-term career growth. * **Stable, Long\-Term Employment** with a well\-established Irish company. * **Positive \& Supportive Workplace** focused on safety, teamwork, and innovation. * **Career Progression Opportunities** within a growing organisation. **Take the Next Step in Your Career** If you’re a motivated fabricator with strong welding expertise, stainless steel experience, and pride in producing high\-quality work, we’d love to hear from you. Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Maintenance Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Maintenance Technician** **Location:** **Grangecastle, Dublin** **Duration:** **Permanent** **Hours:** **39 hours per week (08:00 to 16:30\)** **Overview of the Role:** The Maintenance Technician is responsible for the execution of the Preventive and Corrective maintenance program as per the Client / PPU requirement. **Day to Day of the Role:** * Day\-to\-Day Relationship with the Veolia Site Manager, Client, Shift Lead and Engineering team. * Maintaining and building new relationships across the site among the internal Veolia team, Client, Engineering team and sub\-contractors * Point of contact for escalation of any issues w.r.t. Plant maintenance. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate * Update MTL’s as appropriate * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and Veolia EHS rules and guidelines. * Liaise and Work with the Shift team on all Utilities Equipment * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. (PMO) * Carry out Periodic reviews of EWI/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * Trouble\-shooting and investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures along with the Shift Operation Group. * Identifying, liaising with, and, where appropriate, co\-ordination of third\-party contractors and suppliers on site. * Ensuring Engineering areas, in liaison with Contract Manager and Contract co\-ordinator, facilities and equipment are kept in a clean, safe and serviceable condition, and general housekeeping highest standards are maintained. * Continuous improvement of contract delivery on site **Required Skills for the Role:** * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in Mechanical / Building Services engineering. * Engineering degree/diploma or equivalent, preferably mechanical/environmental, with two to three years post graduate experience, preferably in mechanical/building services, or * Marine qualification, with 2 to 4 years’ experience at sea. (Desirable but not necessary) **Essential:** * Mechanical / Building Services / Utilities experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. * Pumps, HVAC Systems, Air Compressors, Boilers, Plant steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW /DCW /LPHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, WFI Water Systems, CIP systems, Refrigeration Systems, Process gases, * Building Management Systems (Scada) * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance / Corrective Maintenance Systems. * Familiarity with Predictive Maintenance (CBM) techniques and their benefits. * Worked within a Computerized Maintenance Management (CMMS) Work Order system environment. (EAMS/SAP) * Work within a documented Permit to Work System, Method Statement and Risk Assessment and COHE methods. * Computer literate and good organizational skills * Ability to read and understand P\&ID’s. * Understanding of SOP’s/EWI’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Troubleshooting / repair of Mechanical/Building Services systems * Good Initiative and Pro\-active attitude in approach to duties * Self\-motivation, ability to work alone and also as Team Player. * Attention to detail is critical * Methodical \& Analytical approach to work and the ability to communicate to Supervisor and Colleagues * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability. * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Computer Skills (Excel, Word, Client PC Applications etc) **Desirable:** * Experience in the pharmaceutical, Biotech or food industry * Recertification of bio\-Safety cabinets / Fume hoods. Autoclaves. * Analytical laboratory environment experience * Maintenance and troubleshooting experience of the following systems and processes – RO/WFI, Autoclaves, fume\-hoods, bio\-safety cabinets, LAF cabinets; Fire suppression systems; Vacuum. **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Managing Partner
description: Join the leadership team at Antal International, a prestigious and forward\-thinking recruitment network, specialising in providing global recruitment solutions. If you have entrepreneurial aspirations and dream of launching your own business one day, this could be your golden opportunity. **About Us** Founded in 1993, Antal International is a leading executive recruitment firm headquartered in the UK. Our global presence spans across 130\+ countries. **The Opportunity** For the past 30 years, Antal has been fuelling the entrepreneurial passions of potential business owners. Professionals like yourself, from around the world, have utilized our support to establish and run successful recruitment enterprises. With our proven business model and practices, you can monetise your commercial knowledge and black book of contacts. All whilst managing your own lucrative recruitment firm and enjoying significant financial rewards, regardless of prior recruitment experience! **The Criteria** * Background – A minimum of 5 to 10 years of corporate experience * Commitment to full\-time engagement * Willingness to network \& engage with clients directly * Eagerness to learn and grow with a positive mindset * A "can\-do" attitude essential for a fulfilling entrepreneurial journey **The Perks** * Join an esteemed international brand with a solid reputation * Gain entry to over three decades of intellectual property (IP). * With more than 1\.5 million website visitors and over 780,000 candidate registrations in 2023, you can optimize your ability to attract both clients and candidates to your business. * Complete transparency and access to our extensive database of global clients, totalling over 60,000 records. * Connect with a global network of supportive, like\-minded individuals, fostering collaboration and business opportunities * Unlock unlimited earning potential generating substantial revenues of over 120,000 in the first TWELVE MONTHS and achieving work\-life balance * Continuously benefit from support in Training, PR \& Marketing, * Capitalise on Brand Exposure: Showcase yourself, your office, and your expertise to our social media community (with over 850,000 followers on LinkedIn) * In\-depth onboarding and training on the renowned and proven Antal Recruitment Methodology * Continual Personal development with access to interactive online learning modules led by instructors: Permanent access to training and development resources through the Antal Intranet platform. * Assistance with Office Setup and IT Infrastructure: Guidance on establishing your office and meeting IT and telecommunication needs and continuous access to a library of IT resources via the Intranet. * Peer Support Network: Regular Collaborative sessions with offices specializing in various disciplines (such as Finance, Logistics, IT, Sales and Marketing, and Engineering) to discuss clients, and market trends, exchange ideas, offer advice, and share and explore business opportunities together. * Promote your business and benefit by using a leading\-edge chatbot to communicate with clients and candidates, seamlessly integrated across every page of Antal.com. * Benefit from networking opportunities through participation in the Annual Global Conference, which brings together offices from around the world. * Enjoy remote work, flexibility, and autonomy **WE** provide comprehensive training on all the core pillars of International Recruitment and further ongoing support. Take charge of creating and launching your firm, with operational management guidance provided by our experienced team. **YOU** bring your industry expertise and network, and **WE** equip you with everything you need to excel in recruitment and entrepreneurship. Our winning combination includes providing all the tools, resources, coaching, business support, and marketing strategies. **Apply NOW** to explore this opportunity further and embark on the path to business ownership.

title: Marketing Data Analyst
description: **Why Arup?** At Arup we’re dedicated to shaping a better world. Our purpose, shared values and collaborative approach have set us apart for over 80 years. We solve the world's most complex problems and deliver what seems impossible, with curiosity and creativity. We’re a thriving and growing organisation which offers you many possibilities to shape a better world, and your future. In an increasingly competitive marketplace, our Marketing, Communications, and Business Development (MCBD) team enables Arup to build and protect our reputation, deepen client relationships, win quality work, and attract top talent. This role will be key in achieving our strategic market and client ambitions. We are seeking to hire a Marketing Data Analyst to strengthen our MCBD function. **The Opportunity** This role is within the evolving global business development team. You will be working with business development personnel across the firm, including those responsible for the development of client systems and those running business development programmes such as the client programme, the contractor programme and initiatives identified by the Business and Markets Executive / Business Growth and Client Systems leadership. The winning of work and associated activities are key to the success of Arup, more than ever before. This is an opportunity to be part of the team enabling firm growth. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and assist in the development of all forms of Power BI reporting utilizing CRM, finance and other data for use by the marketing, communications, business development and Business and Markets communities across Arup. * Work with leadership, MCBD and other members of the Client Systems team to define requirements for reporting, to sketch out possible solutions, and to elicit feedback once reporting has been created. * Support the coordination of the Marketing Data Warehouse. Work with our digital marketing experts on our data warehouse that stores data from our digital marketing platform, Marketo, and its evolution to encompass website and social media data. Its aim is to give a complete picture of the operation of our digital marketing channels. * Bring your expertise in reporting to support the development of CRM and other tools so that our data is best configured to produce the reporting we need. * Explore the use of AI to support development of reporting solutions as well as generate insights into our business growth department. * Collaborate with the wider ARUP’s analytics community in predictive modeling and statistical analysis projects that would help surface insights around improving our work\-winning processes. **Is this role right for me?** **Requirements** * Experience in the use of Power BI plus high\-level Excel skills. Desire to grow these skills. Potential interest in languages such as Python and tools such as Databricks and SQL querying. * Experience working with CRM and/or finance data. Knowledge of how to clean data prior to processing. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. Ability to think laterally to suggest potential solutions. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal. **Qualifications And Accreditations** * Degree, postgraduate qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, Knowledge Management or a related field. **Knowledge And Experience** * Experience in data management, knowledge management and analytics. * Knowledge of client systems and software development. * Proficiency in marketing and communications related technologies, and knowledge of marketing data including bidding and clients data. * Experience in building trusted relationships with internal stakeholders. * Knowledge of current trends in AI, data information, machine learning and data quality is a plus. **Skills And Attributes** * Skills in strategic thinking and analysis and problem\-solving. * Communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment. * Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities. * Able to build collaborative and inclusive working relationships with peers, leaders and other stakeholders. * High level of personal integrity, professionalism, and inclusive leadership. * Commitment to exemplifying Arup’s aims, particularly those of being a ‘humane organisation’, maintaining ‘quality of work’, and ‘straight and honourable dealings. **What We Offer You** At Arup, we care about each member’s success, so we can grow together. Guided by our values, we provide an attractive total reward package that recognizes the contribution of each of our members to our shared success. As well as competitive, fair and equitable pay, we offer a career in which all of our members can belong, grow and thrive – through benefits that support health and wellbeing, a wide range of learning opportunities and many possibilities to have an impact through the work they do. We are owned in trust on behalf of our members, giving us the freedom, with personal responsibility, to set our own direction and choose work that aligns with our purpose and adds to Arup’s legacy. Our members collaborate on ambitious projects to deliver remarkable outcomes for our clients and communities. Profit Share is a key part of our reward, enabling members to share in the results of our collective efforts. We also provide Private medical insurance, Life assurance, Accident insurance and Income protection cover. In addition, you’ll have access to flexible benefits to help you look after all aspects of your wellbeing and give you the freedom and flexibility to find the best solutions for you, your family, and your individual needs. **Different People, Shared Values** Arup is an equal opportunity employer that actively promotes and nurtures a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, marital status, disability, neurotype or mental health, race or ethnicity, faith or belief, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and whether you’re pregnant or on family leave. We are an open environment that embraces diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas – this drives our excellence. Guided by our values and alignment with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, we create and contribute to equitable spaces and systems, while cultivating a sense of belonging for all. Our internal employee networks support our inclusive culture: from race, ethnicity and cross\-cultural working to gender equity and LGBTQ\+ and disability inclusion – we aim to create a space for you to express yourself and make a positive difference. Discover more about life at Arup at www.arup.com/careers/your\-life\-at\-arup. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible to all candidates. Please contact nick.newell@arup.com to let us know if you need any assistance or reasonable adjustments throughout your application or interview process, and/or to perform the essential functions of the role. We will do everything we can to support you. **Our Application Process** To understand what to expect next, please visit: https://www.arup.com/careers/recruitment\-process/ Stay safe online – Arup will never ask for your bank details as part of our recruitment process. Recruitment Agencies – We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on the list.

title: Marketing Technology Analyst
description: No Relocation Assistance Offered Job Number \#172769 \- Dublin, Dublin, Ireland **Who We Are** Colgate\-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name! Join Colgate\-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all. **Marketing Technology Analyst, Digital Marketing GIT EMEA** **Help us transform the digital experience for pets and the people who care for them.** We are looking for a high\-energy **Marketing Technology** **Analyst** to join our GIT EMEA team. You will **lead** technology enablement for Data Acquisition, Marketing Automation and Omni\-Channel Personalisation for Hill’s EMEA across the pet and vet ecosystem. We aim to create a **seamless, personalized digital experience** that supports veterinarians in their practice and empowers pet parents in their journey. By harmonizing data across every touchpoint, we ensure that every interaction—whether in a clinic or on a smartphone—is relevant, timely, and impactful. **The Mission** 1P CRM data acquisition and activation is a key strategic driver for Hill’s EMEA. You will be the bridge between cutting\-edge technology and real\-world business value, working at the center of the Hill’s EMEA team, as part of the Global IT team, to craft a world\-class digital journey for vets and pet parents. **What You’ll Do** * Master the Data Lifecycle: Maintain a deep, end\-to\-end understanding of data flows across the entire ecosystem. You will ensure that data moves seamlessly between platforms with zero loss, maintaining high integrity from ingestion to activation. * Partner with Global Digital Applications teams to implement MarTech solutions across the entire professional and consumer journey. * Partner with the Hill’s EMEA team to drive acquisition, activation, and harmonization of first\-party data to fuel hyper\-personalized engagement. * Empower Local Markets: You’ll guide local markets on leveraging global platforms while ensuring their specific requirements are translated into technical reality. * Partner with CRM Managers: Work hand\-in\-hand with CRM leads to translate strategic objectives into technical requirements, ensuring data, journeys, and campaigns are built for success. * Be the Tech Ambassador: Translate "tech\-speak" into business value, driving tool adoption and ensuring stakeholders are aligned and inspired. * Safeguard the Experience: Lead regression testing across the ecosystem to ensure technical updates never compromise the user experience. * Document \& Train: Create the "source of truth" documentation and training materials to clarify roles and keep technical knowledge flowing across teams. * Manage the Flow: Navigate agile projects with ease, managing multiple assignments without losing sight of the details. **Who You Are** * Marketing\-Centric: You have a proven track record in Marketing, CRM and Media projects. You understand the "why" behind a campaign and how to speak the language of brand managers. * Technical Specialist: You are hands\-on with data modeling, complex analysis, and activation workflows. You don't just see data; you see the architecture required to move it. * A Solutions Hunter: You don’t just find bugs; you find better ways to work. You are self\-motivated, comfortable with ambiguity, and love to troubleshoot and experiment. * A Master Communicator: You can explain a roadmap to a developer and a business case to a Director with equal clarity. * Technically Sharp: You understand the "how" behind the "what," especially regarding data ingestion and harmonized ecosystems. **Your Toolkit** * Education: Degree in Business, IT, Computer Science, or Engineering. * Tech Literacy: Solid grasp of APIs, data modeling, data analysis, workflow and the digital marketing ecosystem. * Agile Mindset: Proven track record of managing digital initiatives and technology enablement in an agile environment. **Bonus Points For:** * A background in Email Marketing and activation strategy. * Hands\-on experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Salesforce Data360, Gigya (Customer Data Cloud), and Snowflake. * Experience with CDP, Adobe Target, AEM, GA4 * Experience with Clean rooms; Infosum, Liveramp, Amazon **Knowledge of SQL, JavaScript, HTML, or CSS.** **Our Commitment to Inclusion** Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business. **Equal Opportunity Employer** Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law. Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.

title: Mechanical Electrical Engineer
description: **Title:** Mechanical \& Electrical Engineers / Designers (Multiple Roles) **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid options available) **Type:** 12\-Month Contract **Salary:** DOE **Ref:** DES **Overview** We are partnering with a leading engineering consultancy in Dublin to support a range of **complex, high\-tech projects** across semiconductor manufacturing, data centres, and advanced industrial facilities. Due to continued project demand, there are **multiple contract opportunities available** for both Engineers and Designers across Mechanical (HVAC) and Electrical disciplines. These roles offer exposure to the **full project lifecycle** , from concept and design through to construction support, within a highly collaborative, multi\-disciplinary environment. **Open Positions** * Mechanical / HVAC Engineer * Mechanical / HVAC Designer * Electrical Engineer * Electrical Designer **Key Responsibilities (will vary by role)** * Support or lead design activities from concept through to detailed design and construction * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams (mechanical, electrical, structural, architectural, process) * Develop and review 2D/3D designs using BIM tools such as Revit and AutoCAD * Ensure compliance with industry standards, codes, and project requirements * Contribute to design reviews, risk assessments, and project planning * Support site activities, including surveys and construction observation * Mentor junior team members where applicable **Requirements** * Relevant qualification in Mechanical, Electrical, or Building Services Engineering (or similar) * Experience in design within industrial, semiconductor, data centre, or advanced manufacturing environments * Designers: 3 \- 6\+ years’ experience with strong Revit/AutoCAD skills * Engineers: 5\+ years’ experience with strong design, coordination, and project delivery experience * Strong understanding of HVAC or Electrical systems design (depending on discipline) * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills **Desirable Experience** * Experience working in BIM environments (Navisworks or similar) * Exposure to large\-scale or technically complex projects * Familiarity with design tools such as ETAP, EasyPower, Carrier HAP, or Trane Trace **Only candidates currently based in Ireland with a valid work visa or EU passport will be considered.** **For more information, contact Corné at 0949316002 or corne@dillones.ie.**

title: Moving & Storage Removals Porters & Drivers
description: **Removal Porters and Drivers work in the removals and storage industry, helping customers move household or commercial goods safely from one location to another. They typically work as part of a team handling packing, lifting, transport, and storage operations.** Key Duties \& Responsibilities 1\. Packing \& Preparation Pack, wrap, and protect furniture, equipment, and personal belongings Label and organise items for transport or storage Use packing materials and tools safely Prepare goods for long\- or short\-term storage 2\. Loading \& Unloading Lift and carry heavy items (furniture, boxes, appliances) Load and unload vehicles efficiently and safely Secure items in vehicles to prevent damage during transit Work as part of a crew to complete moves on time 3\. Transportation (Drivers) Drive vans or lorries (applicable licences required) to customer locations Plan routes and ensure timely deliveries Ensure vehicle safety, cleanliness, and legal compliance Assist with loading/unloading when required 4\. Storage Operations Move goods into and out of storage facilities Stack and store items safely in warehouses Maintain inventory records where required Handle specialist storage projects (e.g., offices, schools, hospitals) 5\. Customer Service Interact professionally with customers at homes or business sites Understand customer needs and provide careful handling of belongings Represent the company in a courteous and reliable manner 6\. Health \& Safety Follow manual handling and lifting procedures Use protective equipment when required Ensure safe working practices at all times Prevent damage to property and goods Skills \& Requirements Essential Skills Physical fitness and ability to lift heavy items Teamwork and communication Attention to detail and care with valuables Time management and reliability Experienced at handling fragile quality furniture For Drivers Valid driving licence (often category B, C1, or C) Safe driving record Basic vehicle maintenance awareness Working Conditions Early starts and long or flexible hours Indoor (warehouse) and outdoor (customer sites) work Travel locally or nationally Physically demanding, hands\-on role Summary A Removal Porter/Driver is a hands\-on logistics role combining manual handling, teamwork, driving, and customer service. The job is essential for ensuring smooth, safe, and efficient moving and storage operations for both residential and commercial clients. Pay: €15\.00\-€16\.00 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Network Engineer V - DWDM
description: ***Please note \- you must have valid working rights in Ireland and be able to prove same. There is no sponsorship available for this position. You must be based in Ireland, or be willing to relocate to be considered.*** Are you an accomplished **Optical** **Network Engineer** ready to take on a high\-impact role in a world\-renowned tech organisation? Our client is seeking a seasoned professional to join their elite networking team for a **12\-month onsite contract** in the heart of Dublin. You’ll play a pivotal role maintaining and evolving mission\-critical IP infrastructure that supports operations on a global scale. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design, build and rollout our global DWDM networks; * Drive scaling designs to meet the demand of our customers; * Create processes that help operate and build our optical network; * Work closely with other Networking teams; * Evaluate new products and drive commercial tender process; * Ensure that the optical network adheres to the defined network standards; * Review and implement changes on the optical systems; * Drive operational excellence projects to drive improvements in our DWDM network quality and reliability; * Troubleshoot complex problems and develop innovative solutions day to day on our network. **Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s Degree in a technology related field or equivalent experience to a Bachelor's degree; * Minimum of 7\+ years industry experience; * Experience with optical planning, modelling tools and rollout of large DWDM networks; * Expertise IP networking fundamentals and IP protocols; * In\-depth knowledge of and experience with major DWDM platforms (Ciena, Infinera and Cisco); * In\-depth knowledge of and experience with major router platforms (Cisco and Juniper) including compatible supervisory and line cards for these platforms; * In\-depth knowledge of and experience with major internet routing protocols; specifically BGP and OSPF; * Excellent network analysis fundamentals and robust troubleshooting skills; * Candidates should also have had significant past experience with, and expertise in many of the following protocols \& technologies: DWDM, T1/DS1, DS3, SONET (OC3, OC48\), 802\.2/3, 802\.1d VLANs/STP, IPv4 \& IPv6, TCP (internals \& flow control), BGP, OSPF, HSRP/GLBP, PIMv2, IGMP, LDP, TACACS, IPSEC \& VPNs, netflow, DNS, HTTP;

title: Network Engineer, Operations & Support
description: Meta's Edge \& Network Service team is actively looking for people to help us tackle the unique set of challenges and opportunities we face, while contributing to our company mission of connecting the world. Our team is the execution arm of the Meta network team and is responsible for the operations and support of all production networks (Edge, Backbone, Optical, and Labs) as well as our Content Delivery Network across the globe. This truly global team offers a unique career opportunity to work with all of the latest network technologies and engineers solving some of the most complex problems in the industry. Network Engineer, Operations \& Support Responsibilities: * Incident Response: Drive work investigating complex technical and process issues on a global scale spanning multiple reliability, security, and continuity disciplines for infrastructure spanning thousands of locations during major incidents/site events on edge, caching, and network infrastructure. This will require you to work closely and effectively with a variety of cross functional teams, managed service providers, and third\-party vendor partners * Escalation Management: Participate in the global team’s Tier 3 and 4 on\-call rotation with the goal of routing issues as needed and understanding how processes or tooling might be improved, skills can be developed, or automation can be implemented to prevent the need to escalate similar issues in the future * Risk Management and Assurance: Work internally and with upstream partner teams to ensure design, build, and operations aligns to applicable reliability, security, privacy, regulatory policy, and business continuity drivers * Information and Data Assurance: ensure relevant operational process, procedure, and policy documentation is effectively managed and the data required to support operations is complete and accurate in systems * Automation: Be heavily involved in driving the team to analyze operational events in order to identify new automation opportunities and help us achieve our goal of all faults in the network being fully remediated by software. This will include helping others understand our requirements and drive their roadmaps, but may include directly implementing light weight solutions in code * Data Measurement: As an operations practitioner supporting our network, you will be expected to drive quality into the metrics we report to assist us in focusing on the areas that give us the best return on investment. This could include measurement and analysis of our escalation issues, fault/event trends, infrastructure capacity, and vendor performance failures * Operational Leadership: As an operations practitioner within the team you will be expected to drive improvement in everything we do. In this role you will indirectly manage the activities of a large contingent workforce responsible for delivering road mapped projects and executing on recurring activities. You will drive standards across the network and ensure that we are fully compliant to those standards and policies Minimum Qualifications: * Network Protocol: knowledge of TCP/IP, IPv4/v6, Border Gateway Protocol, Intermediate System to Intermediate System, Open Shortest Path First, and/or Multi\-protocol Label Switching in complex troubleshooting scenarios. Cisco and Juniper Professional level or equivalent experience * Information Technology Functional Experience: Minimum of 10 years working within global network or infrastructure operations, deployment, design/engineering and/or support teams * Vendor Partner Experience: Minimum of 10 years experience partnering to translate performance issues into improvement plans with enterprise and service provider vendors such as network hardware platforms(Cisco, Juniper, Ciena, Infinera, Nokia, and Arista), ITAD vendors, logistics vendors, and colocation vendors * Business Metrics: Experience identifying key metrics/measures that will be used to evaluate success and validate the business impact of the program * Professional Services: Experience with purchasing, negotiating and end\-to\-end supplier management, such as managing global RFPs and contract negotiations * Partnership: Experience successfully collaborating across a global team and with cross\-functional partners(e.g. physical infrastructure \& network design, engineering, strategy, security, policy, and legal) at all levels to include vendor service delivery managers, individual contributors, and people managers * Prioritization: Experience managing from the front to prioritize and drive the bigger mission forward by translating strategy into results * Automation: Basic knowledge coding and automating in higher\-level languages such as Python, Go, or JavaScript Preferred Qualifications: * Repair Function: 5 years of logical troubleshooting and physical repair with an understanding of physical infrastructure such as cable types, connector types, optic types, racks, patch panels, power/cooling, hardware components, and facility infrastructure * Software Systems Practitioner: Knowledge and direct experience organizing and executing digital transformation initiatives at the practitioner level * Data Design: Basic knowledge applying data led analysis and leveraging technologies like machine learning and predictive modeling algorithms to solve complex business problems * Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements) * Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews) * Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies About Meta: Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics. Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

title: O&M Technician
description: Born from the expertise of teams who have led EPC and O\&M services at Voltalia since 2005, **Renvolt** delivers high\-performance service solutions to accelerate the global energy transition. We specialize in **solar and storage** , operating as a trusted global provider with a strong international track record. With local teams in **14 countries** , more than **400 employees** worldwide, and 24/7 support, we partner with clients across all regions to ensure success. Renvolt is supported by a strong investment group that provides long\-term stability and reinforces our commitment to sustainable growth. Our portfolio includes **5\.6 GW built and under construction** , and **1\.9 GW already in operation** , demonstrating our capability and reliability in delivering large\-scale renewable projects. We prioritize long\-term partnerships grounded in **transparency, quality, reliability, ethics, and integrity** . Join us and be part of a company driving sustainable energy forward. At Renvolt, we welcome individuals from all backgrounds, whatever your race, ethnicity, gender, nationality, age, disability, sexual orientation, and experience. In our company, diversity drives innovation and contributes to a workplace where everyone can thrive. Ready to make an impact? Bring your energy—let’s power a sustainable future together! Renvolt is a subsidiary of Voltalia Group. Find out more about Renvolt at renvolt.energy **YOUR MISSIONS** Renvolt is Growing – Join Our O\&M Team in Ireland! ☀️ Renvolt continues to expand its Operations \& Maintenance (O\&M) presence across Ireland, strengthening the long\-term performance and reliability of our solar assets. We are seeking skilled professionals located near our sites, preferably in the **Carlow** area, to ensure strong local support, fast response times, and efficient on\-site operations. Reporting to the O\&M Site Manager, the **O\&M Technician** has the following responsibilities: * Provide fast on\-site and remote troubleshooting, recommend and implement fault recovery solutions for production re\-establishment; * Carry out on\-site specialized technical maintenance activities; * Participate in Integrated Commissioning \& Start\-up activities; * Carry out on\-site technical assessments; * Provide technical reports and evidence to support claiming processes; * Provide training and technical workshops to spread and consolidate maintenance competences and skills; * Participate in the development of maintenance management processes and systems; * Produce technical documentation to support the maintenance activity; * Recommend/ study more effective and efficient maintenance practices and tools; * Disseminate good practices and standards; * Contribute to the development of reliability studies and failures analysis; * Participate in monitoring and auditing implemented processes, procedures and activities. **QUALIFICATIONS \& EXPERIENCES** The ideal candidate will be/have: * Electrical qualification; * Experience in commissioning and maintenance of photovoltaic power plants (preferred); * Experience in electronics and automation fault detection techniques and troubleshooting; * Maintenance Management \- Standards and Continues Improvement Process; * Experience in SCADA and remote\-control systems; * Experience in data acquisition and communications systems (preferred); * Advanced knowledge of MS Office and reporting software; * Advanced English reading/writing/speaking (mandatory); * Be able to do physical site work; * Must have a valid Irish driver license. **KEY SKILLS** * Initiative, proactivity, autonomy, sense of responsibility; * Ability to motivate and persuade; * Ability to manage stress and handle deliveries under pressure; * Ability to create, defend solutions and implement them; * Organization and method; * Practical sense and pragmatism; * Critical spirit.

title: Office & Facilities Operations Executive
description: **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About The Office \& Facilities Operations Executive:** The Office \& Facilities Operations Executive is a hands\-on, individual contributor role responsible for the day\-to\-day running of the Dublin office. The role is primarily focused on facilities and workplace operations (approximately 85%), with a smaller element of administrative and executive support (approximately 15%). This role suits someone with prior office or facilities experience who is comfortable working independently, coordinating vendors and stakeholders, and keeping the workplace running smoothly, safely, and professionally. **Role \& Responsibilities:** Facilities \& Workplace Operations (85%) * Manage the day\-to\-day operation of the Dublin office, ensuring a safe, professional, and well\-maintained working environment. * Coordinate cleaning, maintenance, building management systems (BMS), workspace setup, and general office upkeep, including monitoring stock levels of office supplies and consumables. * Act as the main operational point of contact for landlords, building management, and external service providers. * Coordinate vendor activities, manage service providers, track service levels (SLAs), and support facilities\-related procurement. * Track facilities\-related budgets, invoices, and routine operational spend, escalating variances where needed. * Support and coordinate Health \& Safety activities, including maintaining documentation, supporting risk assessments, coordinating incident reporting, and escalating issues in line with company policies and statutory requirements. * Act as a facilities contact during building incidents or emergencies (e.g. fire alarms, building issues), coordinating with building management and internal stakeholders. * Serve as a trained First Aider and Fire Warden (training will be provided) and support ongoing workplace safety and wellbeing initiatives. * Coordinate IT and workplace equipment needs with internal IT teams, including workstation setup and basic asset tracking. * Resolve day\-to\-day facilities or operational issues promptly, escalating more complex matters where appropriate. * Manage visitor access in line with Facilities SOPs (pre\-visit, check\-in, post\-visit follow\-up) and coordinate catering when required. * Work closely with the Information Security team to support physical security controls, including identifying and escalating physical security issues. * Provide regular operational updates to the Facilities Leadership team, covering completed and upcoming works, vendor performance, incidents, and spend. Administrative \& Executive Support (15%) * Support new\-hire onboarding, including workspace setup, access badges, induction coordination, and equipment preparation, in line with internal data protection policies. * Respond to employee queries related to office and facilities matters. * Provide ad\-hoc administrative support to executives, such as meeting coordination, travel bookings, and calendar assistance, where business needs require. * Support office culture and engagement activities, including events and team initiatives. * Maintain facilities and office records, trackers, and documentation to support smooth operations and audit readiness. * Assist with occasional executive schedule changes to accommodate client meetings. **Skills \& Qualifications:** * Previous experience in office management, facilities coordination, or workplace operations. * Proven experience coordinating vendors and day\-to\-day facilities services. * Good working knowledge of workplace Health \& Safety practices in Ireland; experience supporting compliance activities in a regulated environment is desirable. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills, with a practical, hands\-on approach. * Comfortable working independently and managing competing priorities. * Clear communicator with the ability to handle sensitive information discreetly. * Experience supporting onboarding and office logistics. * Fluent in spoken and written English. **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Company Pension Plan * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Education reimbursement * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €40,000 \- €45,000* . *There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* *MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual with a disability and require reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* people.success@marketstar.com *for assistance.*

title: Office Administrator/Receptionist
company: SRS Motors, Newtown Tyre & Service Centre
description: **Receptionist/Office Administrator** **Location:** Newtownmountkennedy, Co. Wicklow (In\-Person) **Position:** Part\-time; 20\-28 hours p/w **Salary:** €17,160 – €24,024 **About the Role** Are you a warm, organised professional who takes pride in providing a first\-class customer experience? We are looking for a dedicated **Receptionist / Office Administrator** to join our team in Newtownmountkennedy. Although not essential, the ideal candidate will have a background and experience working within the Motor Trade. As the first point of contact for our clients, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring our operations run smoothly and our customers feel valued from the moment they arrive. **What We Offer** * **Competitive Salary:** Commensurate with experience, with a performance\-based review after Year 1\. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Standard Monday to Friday schedule (no weekends). * **Culture:** A supportive, friendly, and collaborative team environment. * **Growth:** Full training provided with clear opportunities for long\-term career progression and professional development. **Key Responsibilities** * **Client Relations:** Providing a warm and professional welcome to all visitors and managing incoming calls, bookings, and enquiries with excellence. * **Office Operations:** Managing day\-to\-day administration, including invoicing, staff scheduling, inventory management, data entry and documentation. * **Technical Liaison:** Assisting workshop technicians with parts ordering and pricing while ensuring internal KPIs and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are met. * **Environment Management:** Maintaining a clean, organized, and presentable reception and office area. **What You Bring** * **Communication:** Exceptional interpersonal skills and the ability to build rapport with clients and colleagues. * **Tech Savvy:** Proficiency in office software; experience with **ERP or CRM systems** is highly desirable. * **Efficiency:** Strong organizational skills with a "team\-player" mindset and the ability to multitask in a busy environment. * **Experience:** Previous experience in a customer\-facing or admin role (motor industry experience is a significant advantage). * **Reliability:** You must live within a reasonable commuting distance of Newtownmountkennedy. **How to Apply** If you are ready to take the next step in your administrative career within a thriving local business, we would love to hear from you. *Eligible candidates will be contacted for an initial interview. Must have valid work authorisation for Ireland.* Pay: €17,160\.00\-€24,024\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Newtownmountkennedy, County Wicklow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you available to work Monday and Friday 8AM \- 5PM and Tuesday \- Thursday 1PM\-5PM? * Are you available to work Monday \- Friday 1PM \- 5PM? * Are you available to work Monday and Tuesday 8AM \- 5PM and Wednesday \- Friday 1PM \- 5PM? Language: * English, B1/B2 (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Location: * Newtownmountkennedy, County Wicklow (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Operational Security Analyst (IT & Payment Systems)
description: Indra is one of the leading global technology and consulting companies and the technological partner for core business operations of its customers worldwide. It is a world\-leader in providing proprietary solutions in specific segments in Transport and Defence markets, and the leading firm in Digital Transformation Consultancy and Information Technologies in Spain and Latin America through its affiliate Minsait. Its business model is based on a comprehensive range of proprietary products, with a high\-value focus and with a high innovation component. In the 2024 financial year, Indra achieved revenue of € 4\.84 billion, 60,000\+ employees, a local presence in 46 countries and business operations in over 140 countries. As the technological partner for its customers' key operations, Indra is at the core of their business, and Indra's four values guide everything we do: **Innovation** \- Our capacity for innovation, cutting\-edge solutions, and specialised team of professionals enable us to drive a safer, more connected future through technology. **Trust** \- We work with strength, commitment, and reliability, delivering quality solutions to build trust with customers, employees, partners, investors, and society. **Connection** \- We harness the power of collaboration, connect ideas and solutions, and adapt to our customers' needs, supporting them on the path to a better future. **Foresight** \- We anticipate future needs to make the world safer and more connected, transforming our experience and knowledge into solutions for a better tomorrow. **Role Overview** We are seeking an Operational Security Analyst to support the day\-to\-day delivery of information security across our IT, cloud, and payment environments. This role sits within the operations function and is focused on embedding security into live services, ensuring controls are effective, risks are understood, and security practices are consistently applied across engineering and operational teams. The successful candidate will work closely with service delivery, infrastructure, and third\-party providers to support a secure, compliant, and resilient operating environment, particularly within regulated payment (PCI DSS / P2PE) contexts. **Key Responsibilities** * Operational Security \& Service Support * Act as a point of contact for operational security matters, supporting service delivery across cloud, infrastructure, and payment environments * Support the implementation and ongoing operation of security controls across AWS, on\-prem infrastructure, and end\-user environments * Work closely with engineering and operations teams to ensure security is embedded into day\-to\-day activities **Security Operations \& Monitoring** * Support SIEM monitoring and alert triage, identifying and responding to potential security events * Assist in security event investigation and escalation, ensuring appropriate response and documentation * Contribute to improving monitoring coverage and detection capability **Risk \& Vulnerability Management** * Assist in identifying, assessing, and tracking information security risks in collaboration with risk owners * Support vulnerability management processes, including: * Scanning and identification * Prioritisation and remediation tracking * Reporting and follow\-up **Payment Security (PCI / P2PE)** * Support operational security controls within Cardholder Data Environments (CDE) * Assist in maintaining PCI DSS compliance, including: * Logging and monitoring * Access control reviews * Vulnerability management activities * Gain exposure to P2PE environments and device ecosystems, supporting secure operations **Security Controls \& Cryptography** * Assist in the operation of cryptographic controls, with awareness of: * Key management practices * Secure handling and storage * Support access control and identity management processes **Documentation, Standards \& Continuous Improvement** * Contribute to the development and maintenance of: Security procedures and Standards and runbooks * Promote security awareness across teams, ensuring policies are understood and followed * Support automation and continuous improvement initiatives within security operations **Third\-Party \& Compliance Support** * Assist with third\-party security reviews and control validation activities * Support audit and compliance processes, including evidence gathering for: PCI DSS and ISO 27001 * Contribute to maintaining a strong control and governance environment **Requirements** * Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cyber Security, or equivalent practical experience * 1\-3 years' experience in information security, IT operations, or a related technical role **Technical Skills** * Exposure to security operations, including SIEM monitoring or alert triage * Familiarity with vulnerability management tools and processes * Basic understanding of cloud platforms (e.g. AWS) and IT infrastructure **Core Security Knowledge** * Understanding of: Network security fundamentals, Identity and Access Management (IAM, MFA), Endpoint security principles, * Awareness of frameworks such as: ISO 27001, PCI DSS, NIS2 / DORA (desirable) **Desirable Experience** * Exposure to payment security environments (PCI DSS / P2PE) * Experience working in regulated or operational environments **Personal Attributes** * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Good documentation skills and attention to detail * Clear communicator, able to work across technical and non\-technical teams * Proactive mindset with a willingness to learn and take ownership * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment **Benefits** * Holidays: 24 days per annum \+ Irish bank holidays (options to buy/sell days) * Pension \- 7% employee and 7% employer * Private medical insurance (including dental \& optical) * Flexible/hybrid working options * Charitable initiatives * Learning and development programs * Travel \& expense allowances * Innovative \& collaborative work environment *Indra is an equal employment opportunity employer. Applicants are considered without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability or other characteristics protected by law.*

title: Optical Assistant
description: Come join us and make a difference in the world! Discover more at www.necsws.com **Job Description** **This position is a permanent contract for Meath and surrounding counties.** We are hiring for a really great opportunity within our Retinal Technicians team for Meath and surrounding counties. The role involves Eye Photography, used to identify the damage to the retina that can be caused by complications of Diabetes. Experience is not essential, so this could be a really great opportunity for someone looking to take a step into healthcare, or even someone already working in the Healthcare space who would like to develop their skillset. We have a really great, professional team, who will support you every step of the way towards becoming a specialist in this space. **Our Team** You\`ll be part of the national programme, working alongside our Senior Diabetic Retinal Technicians and Team Leader and other programme seniors, whilst being supported by the administration team, programme manager and clinical lead. All of our team are already (or on their way to being) subject matter experts in Retinopathy – so you’ll never find yourself in a position where you don’t have the support you need. As a company, we aim to provide our patients and clients with a consistently high level of care, skill and professional integrity. We believe that the service we provide represents outstanding economic value, yet maintains the highest technical and professional standards clinically achievable. Our commitment extends not just to our patients, but to our staff, our careers and our profession as a whole. The role involves travelling to and from health centres and clinic locations using a company vehicle. **Qualifications** We want to make sure you're set for success, so these are the key skills we\`re looking for: * Experience is not required * **Clean Irish driving license and own car is essential** * A passion for customer care and positive patient outcomes * Being IT literate is a must \- word processing, spreadsheets and regular use of Microsoft Office * Initiative and the ability to prioritise tasks, as well as using problem\-solving skills. * A great work ethic and a proactive approach are essential, as you\`ll be largely unsupervised after the initial training period * Excellent communication skills at all levels **Additional Information** **Benefits:** * Matched Rate Pension * 25 Days Holiday \+ Bank Holidays **Additional:** * Candidates must be able to demonstrate a pre\-existing right to work and travel within the EU. Documentary evidence will be required. * A full, clean Irish driving licence is essential. * All offers are subject to satisfactory vetting and reference checks. Depending on the nature of the role, Garda Vetting may also be required. **NEC Software Solutions is an equal opportunities employer, welcoming applications from all communities. If you require any reasonable adjustments or have specific accessibility needs during the recruitment or interview process, please feel free to share these with us. We are committed to ensuring an inclusive and accommodating experience for all candidates.** **Who We Are:** We’re NEC Software Solutions (part of global tech giant NEC Corporation). While you read this ad, our software is helping to dispatch ambulances, support families, keep trains on the move, locate missing people and even test the hearing of newborn babies. Working with us, you’ll be helping our 3,000\+ employees push the boundaries of what’s possible and support amazing public services. We work with governments, hospitals, police forces, housing providers, local authorities and more. We help them pay financial support faster, speed up treatments for patients and respond to emergencies in the right way. The more we do, the more our customers can do for others. And together, we make a world of difference. We’d love your help. And we’ll support you all the way.

title: Outreach Associate, Integration
description: w2026\_TBC **Open to Internal and External Candidates** Position Title : **Outreach Associate, Integration Project** Duty Station : **Dublin, Ireland** Classification : **General Service Staff, G5** Type of Appointment : **9 months** **Special Short Term Graded Contract, with possibility of extension** Estimated Start Date : **July 2026** Closing Date : **22 May 2026** Established in 1951, IOM is the leading inter\-governmental organization in the field of migration and works closely with governmental, intergovernmental and nongovernmental partners. IOM is dedicated to promoting humane and orderly migration for the benefit of all. It does so by providing services and advice to governments and migrants. ***Context:*** Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission (CoM) and direct supervision of the Senior Outreach Associate, the successful candidate will be responsible for the support requirements of the project assisting Beneficiaries of Temporary Protection (BOTP’s) in Ireland. This role will focus on supporting BOTP’s in Designated Accommodation settings across Ireland, implementing the delivery of orientation sessions, information provision, and offering one\-to\-one assistance. The role will include other programme related responsibilities as required. **Responsibilities and Accountabilities:** 1\. Assist in the implementation and monitoring of project activities. 2\. Retrieve, compile, summarize, analyse, and present information/data on specific project topics. 3\. Support the Senior Outreach Associate with the development and implementation of the Outreach and Information Provision strategy through research and additional support as required. 4\. Deliver information sessions to BOTP’s in Designated Accommodation settings regarding accurate and essential information relevant to their life in Ireland. 5\. Coordinate with assigned focal points at Designated Accommodation Centres to schedule visits and sessions. Maintain coordination with diverse organizational units and external parties to initiate requests, obtain necessary clearances, process and follow\-up on administrative actions, etc. 6\. Disseminate key and accurate information to BOTP’s. Ensure cases with vulnerabilities are identified and have been referred as necessary. Provide translation and interpretation services as necessary. 7\. Draft status reports, identifying shortfalls in delivery, bring them to the attention of the supervisor and suggest remedial actions. 8\. Draft correspondence on project issues; prepare and update reports, briefing notes, graphics, statistical tables, presentation and other forms of documentation. 9\. Respond to complex information requests and inquiries; set up and maintain files/records; organize meetings, workshops and training sessions; 10\. Participate in meetings and conferences; assist in coordinating implementation activities with local authorities, partners, United Nation agencies, intergovernmental and non\-governmental organizations, donors and other stakeholders relevant to the project. 11\. Assist in monitoring work of implementing partners and report non\-compliances to the supervisor. 12\. Provides guidance/training to new/junior staff. 13\. Perform other related duties as assigned. **Required Qualifications and Experience:** **Education:** * School diploma with five years of relevant experience; or, * Bachelor’s degree in Political or Social Sciences, International Relations, Development Studies, Migration Studies, Human Rights, Law or related fields from an accredited academic institution with three years of relevant professional experience. **Experience:** * Experience in disseminating information to large audiences in a wide variety of settings. * Experience in working with migrants, refugees, internally displaced persons, victims of trafficking and other vulnerable groups; and, * Demonstrated proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, including Excel, PowerPoint, Good knowledge of information technology and proficiency in Microsoft Office applications especially Excel, Word, PowerPoint and Publisher * Prior work experience with international humanitarian organizations, non\-government or government institutions/organization in a multi\-cultural setting is an advantage. **Skills:** * In depth knowledge of the broad range of migration related subject areas dealt with by the Organization; and, * Presentation and clear communication skills * A full, clean driving license and access to a personal vehicle is required for this role. **Languages:** * IOM’s official languages are English, French, and Spanish * For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written). * Working knowledge of any UN languages an advantage **Required Competencies:** **Values** * *Inclusion and respect for diversity: R*espects and promotes individual and cultural differences; encourages diversity and inclusion wherever possible. * *Integrity and transparency: M*aintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct. * *Professionalism:* demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day\-to\-day challenges. * *Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance.* * *Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated.* **Core Competencies –** behavioural indicators * *Teamwork:* Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results. * *Delivering results*: Produces and delivers quality results in a service\-oriented and timely manner; is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes. * *Managing and sharing knowledge*: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate. * *Accountability*: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own action and delegated work. * *Communication*: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication ***Other:*** Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this Special vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation. Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment and verification of residency, visa and authorizations by the concerned Government. ***This post is subject to local recruitment. Only persons holding a valid residence and work permit in Ireland will be eligible for consideration.*** ***As the right to live and work in Ireland is required, the contract issued may be shorter than the period mentioned in the advertisement, in order to align with expiry date of the work permit.*** Short‑listed candidates will be invited to a **45‑minute online interview**. ***How to apply:*** Please apply to this job post, sharing your updated CV and Cover Letter explaining your interest in the role. **Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview, soon after the closing of the Vacancy.** ***Posting period:*** From 08\.05\.26 to 22\.05\.26 Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €3,081\.00 per month Benefits: * Additional leave * Free or subsidised travel * Private dental insurance * Private medical insurance * Sick pay Application question(s): * Do you have your own vehicle? Licence/Certification: * A Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Willingness to travel: * 75% (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Part Qualified Accountant- MNC Retail- Dublin West
description: We have an exciting opportunity for a bright Part Qualified Accountant with a luxury Retail/ Distribution multinational based in West Dublin. Acting as the right hand to the Finance Manager, you’ll join a collaborative team of four in a fast\-paced finance environment. This role offers broad exposure across the function, where you’ll play a key part in supporting payables and receivables, while taking ownership of general ledger activities. You’ll also contribute directly to the month\-end close process across multiple entities, gaining valuable insight into the full accounting cycle and helping drive accuracy and efficiency across the team. **Nice to know:** * Hybrid\-3 days in office * Parking onsite, easy access to Train and Luas * 5% Pension and healthcare * Study support available for exams * Quarterly social events * Join a fast\-paced multinational environment **Key Responsibilities:** * Support the Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable functions and workflow of juniors on the team * Prepare and post journal entries, maintaining the fixed asset register, including prepayments and accruals * Perform weekly and monthly bank reconciliations across multiple currencies, investigating and resolving discrepancies * Calculate and process monthly sales commission payments in line with company policies * Prepare and submit VAT returns across multiple entities, ensuring compliance with relevant regulations * Assist with month\-end and year\-end close processes and monthly management accounts, ensuring deadlines are met * Provide support during internal and external audits, including preparation of required documentation * Contribute to wider finance activities and support ad\-hoc projects as required **Person Specification \& Experience:** * Minimum of 3 plus years’ relevant accounting experience * Prior Accounts Receivable or Accounts Payable experience preferred * Experience with month end close and monthly management accounts * Part Qualified Accountant – ACCA/CIMA Etc * Strong Excel / IT
